_templateName: Get chess.com game reviews by email using Google Gemini or other LLMs
_templateDescription:
Improve your chess automatically — get a personalized AI coaching report delivered.
This workflow connects to the free Chess.com public API, fetches your most recent game, and sends it to an AI language model that acts as your personal chess coach. Within minutes, you receive a structured HTML email covering everything from critical turning points to a personalized training plan.

🔧 How It Works

Trigger — Runs on a daily schedule or manually on demand
Config Node — Set your Chess.com username and email address (one-time setup)
Fetch Games — Pulls your current month's games from Chess.com's public API (no API key required)
Sort & Enrich — Identifies your color, result (Win/Loss/Draw), opponent details, ratings, and ECO opening code
AI Analysis — Sends the full PGN and game context to your chosen LLM with a structured coaching prompt
Email Report — Delivers a clean HTML coaching report directly to your inbox via Gmail


📊 What's in the Coaching Report

Game Summary — Opening, middlegame, and endgame narrative
Critical Moments — 3–5 turning points with explanations of what happened and what should have been played
Mistake Categories — Tactical oversights, positional errors, opening issues, endgame technique, time management
What Went Well — Genuine strengths recognized and reinforced
Five Key Lessons — Specific, memorable takeaways tied to your actual game
Training Plan — Targeted drills and study resources based on your weaknesses
Closing Motivation — Encouraging next step to keep you improving


⚙️ Setup (Under 5 Minutes)

Import the workflow into n8n
Open the Config node and enter your Chess.com username and target email address
Connect your Gmail OAuth2 credential
Connect your preferred LLM credential (Google Gemini is pre-wired; swap for OpenAI or Claude if preferred)
Activate the workflow — done!


🤖 Supported AI Models
The quality of the results depends on the model you choose. You can start with the free Gemini models and compare the output with your expected results.

The LLM node is plug-and-play, allowing you to switch between different models with a single click.

Google Gemini 2.5 Flash (pre-configured)
OpenAI GPT-4o — Best overall chess analysis quality
Anthropic Claude Sonnet — Excellent at explanations and coaching tone
Mistral / Llama — Self-hosted options for privacy-conscious users


💡 Stronger models produce better analysis. GPT-4o or Claude Sonnet are recommended for players rated 1000+.


📬 Extend the Workflow
After the email step, you can easily add nodes to also send the report via:

Telegram — instant mobile notifications
Slack — post to a personal or team channel
Notion — build a game review database
Google Drive — archive reports as files
Discord — share with your chess study group


✅ Requirements

n8n (self-hosted or cloud)
A Chess.com account with at least one game this month
Gmail OAuth2 credential
Any supported LLM API credential (Google Gemini, OpenAI, Anthropic, etc.)
name: ♟️ Chess Game Coach — Auto AI Review via Email

nodes.name: ⏰ Daily Schedule Trigger
nodes.notes: Runs once per day. Adjust the interval to your preferred cadence (e.g., every 12 hours for active players).

nodes.name: ▶️ Manual Test Run
nodes.notes: Use this to test the workflow on demand without waiting for the schedule.

parameters.content:
# ♟️ AI Chess Game Coach

An n8n workflow that automatically fetches your latest Chess.com game, analyzes it with AI, and emails you a personalized coaching report.

---

## How It Works

1. Triggers on a daily schedule (or manually on demand)
2. Fetches your most recent game from the Chess.com public API
3. Enriches the game data with player context, result, and opening code
4. Sends the full game to an AI coach for structured analysis
5. Emails you an HTML coaching report covering critical moments, mistakes, strengths, and a training plan

---

## Created by

**Ucartz Online**
For any queries or clarifications about this workflow, feel free to reach out.
📧 [support@ucartz.com](mailto:support@ucartz.com)

## Step 1 — Configure

Open the **⚙️ Config** node and set:

• `username` → your Chess.com handle
• `email` → where to send reports

💡 Username is case-insensitive.

## Steps 2–4 — Fetch & Enrich

**Fetch Games** calls the free Chess.com API (no key needed).

**Sort** orders games newest → oldest.

**Add Context** determines:
- Which color you played
- Your result (Win / Loss / Draw)
- Opponent info & ECO code

All data is passed directly into the AI prompt.

## Step 5 — AI Analysis

The AI coach receives the full PGN, player context, and ratings, then produces a structured HTML report.

**Swap the LLM node** to use:
- OpenAI GPT-4o
- Anthropic Claude
- Mistral, Llama, etc.

💡 Better models → better analysis. GPT-4o or Claude Sonnet recommended for 1000+ rated players.

## Step 6 — Send Report

The HTML report is emailed directly.

**Want other channels?**
Add nodes after the email step:
- **Telegram** — send as message
- **Slack** — post to channel
- **Notion** — save to database
- **Google Drive** — save as file
- **Discord** — post to server

## ♟️ Chess Game Coach - Sample Result

♟️ Game Review — daniel-albin
Playing as: White (829)  |  Opponent: mary_elliee00 (833)
Result: 💔 Loss (timeout)  |  Time Control: 60s Bullet
Opening: B00 — Nimzowitsch Defense  |  View Game →

📊 Game Summary
You started the game well, immediately challenging the center with 1.e4 and 2.d4. The early queen exchange on move 6 simplified the position significantly, shifting the focus from attacking chess to piece coordination...

🎯 Critical Moments
Move 3 — Early capture relieves tension
Capturing with 3.dxe5 resolved the central tension early, allowing Black to develop smoothly with ...Nxe5. Holding the tension longer often creates more attacking chances.

Move 6 — Queen exchange changes the game
After Qxd8+ Kxd8, the character of the game changes completely. Black's king becomes active in the center — a strength in simplified positions.

💡 Five Key Lessons
Don't rush to capture pawns unless you gain a clear advantage.
Exchanging queens early means your game plan must change accordingly.
In bullet chess, clock management is as important as move quality.
After piece trades, the active king can be a strength — or a danger.
Identify the decisive moment: what one move changed the game?

💪 Closing Motivation
You're already applying solid opening principles — that's a great foundation! Focus on time management in your next bullet session and you'll feel the improvement immediately. Keep going! 🚀

nodes.name: Add Player Context
nodes.notes:
Enriches each game with perspective-aware data:
• Which color did the reviewing player use?
• What was the result from their perspective?
• Opponent name, ratings, and ECO opening code

This context is passed directly into the AI prompt.

nodes.name: Sort Games (Latest First)
nodes.notes: Sorts the game list by end time descending so the most recent game is processed first. Also injects the reviewing username into each game record.

nodes.name: Fetch Games from Chess.com
nodes.notes: Calls the Chess.com public API to retrieve all games played this month. Returns a JSON object with a 'games' array.

nodes.name: Build API URL (Current Month)
nodes.notes: Constructs the Chess.com public API endpoint for the current calendar month. No API key required — Chess.com's game archive is publicly accessible.

nodes.name: Process Last Game Only
nodes.notes:
Processes only the most recent game (batch size = 1 by default). The loop exits after the first game.

To analyze multiple recent games, remove this node and let all items flow through — but be mindful of AI API costs.

nodes.name: Config  - Username - Email
nodes.notes:
🔧 CONFIGURE THIS NODE FIRST

Set your Chess.com username (case-insensitive) and the email address where you want to receive your AI coaching reports.

nodes.name: AI Chess Coach (Analysis)
nodes.notes:
The core AI analysis node. Sends the enriched game data to your chosen LLM with a detailed coaching prompt.

The prompt is structured for beginner-to-intermediate players (~600–1400 rating). Adjust the tone or depth in the System Message if needed.

parameters.text:
=# Chess Game Analysis Request

## Player Info
- **Reviewing Player**: {{ $json.reviewingUsername }} ({{ $json.playerColor }}, Rated {{ $json.playerRating }})
- **Opponent**: {{ $json.opponentUsername }} ({{ $json.opponentColor }}, Rated {{ $json.opponentRating }})
- **Result**: {{ $json.resultEmoji }} {{ $json.gameResult }}
- **Time Control**: {{ $json.time_control }} ({{ $json.time_class }})
- **Opening (ECO)**: {{ $json.ecoCode }}
- **Game Link**: {{ $json.url }}
- **Player Result Code**: {{ $json.playerResult }}
- **Opponent Result Code**: {{ $json.opponentResult }}

## Position & Game Record
**Final FEN**: `{{ $json.fen }}`

**PGN**:
```
{{ $json.pgn }}
```

---

## Analysis Instructions

Analyze this game from {{ $json.playerColor }}'s perspective ({{ $json.reviewingUsername }}, ~{{ $json.playerRating }} rating).

Produce a complete coaching report in **clean HTML** (ready for Gmail) using the exact structure below.

---

### 1. 📊 Game Summary

Include a header block with all player info, result, time control, opening code, and a [View Game] hyperlink.

Then write **2–3 paragraphs** covering:
- Opening phase evaluation
- Middlegame key developments
- Endgame progression (if applicable)
- The decisive moment(s)

---

### 2. 🎯 Critical Moments (3–5 turning points)

For each moment:
- Clearly describe the position and what move was played
- Explain what should have been done instead
- State the **underlying concept or pattern**

---

### 3. ❌ Mistake Categories

Group errors under these headings (only include relevant ones):
- **Tactical Oversights** — Missing captures, checks, threats
- **Positional Errors** — Weak squares, piece placement, pawn structure
- **Opening Issues** — Principle violations or theory deviations
- **Endgame Technique** — Conversion or defensive mistakes
- **Time Management** — If it influenced the result

---

### 4. ✅ What Went Well (2–4 highlights)

Recognize genuine strengths — good tactics, solid positional choices, fighting spirit.

---

### 5. 💡 Five Key Lessons

Exactly 5 specific, memorable takeaways with context tied to THIS game.

---

### 6. 📚 Training Plan

- **Primary Focus**: Main weakness to address
- **Drill 1**: Specific exercise
- **Drill 2**: Specific exercise
- **Drill 3**: Specific exercise
- **Study Resources**: Concrete recommendations (videos, books, Chess.com tools)

---

### 7. 💪 Closing Motivation

1–2 encouraging sentences with a clear, actionable next step.

options.systemMessage:
# Chess Coach AI — System Prompt You are an experienced, encouraging chess coach who reviews games for club-level players rated 600–1400. ## Your Voice - Warm, clear, and conversational English - Always constructive — never harsh or discouraging - Explain WHY moves work, not just that they do - Use simple language; briefly explain any chess jargon on first use ## Analysis Philosophy - Prioritize **big-picture understanding** over engine-perfect lines - Focus on **recurring patterns and themes**, not isolated blunders - Emphasize **practical improvement** the player can apply next game - Balance every critique with genuine positive reinforcement ## What to Avoid - Long engine variations (e.g., "After 15...Nxe4 16.Bxf7+ Ke8 17.Bd5...") - Heavy opening theory jargon - Nitpicking minor inaccuracies in winning positions - Discouraging or condescending language ## Output Format — STRICT HTML for Gmail Use ONLY these HTML elements: - `

` and `

` for section headers - `

` for paragraphs - `

    ` and `
  • ` for bullet lists - `
      ` and `
    1. ` for numbered lists - `` for bold emphasis - `` for italics - `` for links - `
      ` for line breaks - `
      ` for section dividers - `
      ` for key takeaways DO NOT use: - Markdown syntax (no **, ##, -, *, ~~~) - Inline CSS styles - Tables (poor Gmail rendering) - Images or external assets ## Philosophy Every game, win or lose, is a lesson. Help the player understand what happened, why it happened, and exactly what to practice next. Leave them excited to play their next game.

nodes.name: LLM — Google Gemini
nodes.notes:
🔧 SWAP THIS NODE to use a different AI model.

Recommended alternatives:
• OpenAI GPT-4o — Best overall chess analysis quality
• Anthropic Claude 3.5 Sonnet — Excellent explanations
• Google Gemini 1.5 Pro — Good balance of speed and quality

Higher-rated players (1200+) benefit most from stronger models.

nodes.name: Email the Report
nodes.notes:
Sends the AI coaching report as a formatted HTML email.

The subject line includes the player name, result, and color played for easy inbox scanning.

To forward to other channels (Telegram, WhatsApp, Slack), add the appropriate node after this one.

parameters.subject: =♟️ Chess Game Review — {{ $('Process Last Game Only').item.json.reviewingUsername }}
